name: Georgian Cheese Pastries
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Pastry: Combine flour, salt, cream of tartar and butter in a food processor. Process until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Gradually add water, processing just until combined. Place on plastic wrap, flatten into a disk, and wrap tightly. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour or up to 30 days.
2. Roll dough on a floured surface to a thickness of 1/8 inch. Cut out 24 circles using a 3-inch round cookie cutter, or trace around a 3-inch water glass. Pleat the edges of the circles and place them in muffin tins.
3. Prepare Filling: Beat egg in a bowl. Mix Muenster, Cheddar, Parmesan, parsley, chives, and mint. Spoon about 2 teaspoons of filling into each pastry shell and spread evenly (filling can be prepared up to 3 hours ahead and refrigerated).
4. Bake in a preheated hot oven (425°F) for 12-15 minutes or until the filling is golden brown and the pastry begins to color. Remove from tins; serve on a rack.
